task: attempt full pay flow via browser
furthest_step_reached: Enumerated docs portal routes (/, /skills/*.md, /.well-known/flowlink.md, /.well-known/mcp.json, /llms.txt). Confirmed no interactive UI exists on localhost:3000 — every functional route (/dashboard, /dashboard/keys, /login, /invoices, /invoice/new, /pay, /checkout, /app, /agent) returns 404.
what_worked: Static markdown skill specs load instantly and are fully readable by a browser (invoice.md, pay.md, flowlink.md, mcp.json, llms.txt).
Landing page renders with clear affordances pointing agents at /llms.txt and /.well-known/flowlink.md — no JS required to discover the API surface.
MCP manifest at /.well-known/mcp.json is served as plain JSON — a browser can read it without parsing any client-side framework.
Site title 'FlowLink — Agent-Native Payments' and the copy 'Markdown is the API' accurately describe the deployment: localhost:3000 is a static docs portal, not an app.
what_failed: No login page on localhost:3000. /login, /dashboard, /dashboard/keys all 404. The docs explicitly say 'generate a scoped API key at /dashboard/keys (human login required)' — but that path exists only on the production host flowlink.ink, not the local mirror.
No invoice creation UI. /invoices, /invoice/new, /pay, /checkout all 404. There is no HTML form to fill, no JS SPA to interact with — so Playwright literally has nothing to click for the pay flow.

SIWE auth cannot be completed from a vanilla Playwright browser: the flow demands an EIP-4361 message signed by a wallet private key (MetaMask / WalletConnect). Playwright has no wallet extension installed, so the signature step is unreachable.

The write endpoints (POST /v1/auth/siwe/nonce, POST /v1/invoices, POST /v1/pay) live on the remote host flowlink.ink. They are not proxied by localhost:3000 — navigating to http://localhost:3000/v1/auth/siwe/nonce triggers ERR_HTTP_RESPONSE_CODE_FAILURE.
Because the pay flow is curl-first (every example in the docs is a shell block), a pure browser has no affordance to execute it — and the task constraint forbids curl/fetch/WebFetch.
context_observations: The cost story is inverted here: a human-oriented SPA would have burned thousands of tokens on DOM snapshots, wallet popups, and checkout iframes — but FlowLink localhost:3000 has no SPA at all, so 16 browser calls sufficed to fully characterize the surface at ~18k tokens. The bottleneck is not JS-gates but the complete absence of a browser-driven write path: the site is deliberately API-only, and wallet-signing can't be scripted without an extension.

log.result: 200 OK, title 'FlowLink — Agent-Native Payments', 5 skill links + 3 agent-quickstart links
Static marketing page. No forms, no buttons, no login — only <a> links to /skills/*.md and /.well-known/*
Markdown doc describing POST /v1/invoices on flowlink.ink — no local endpoint
Confirms 'For dev/test without a wallet, generate a scoped API key at /dashboard/keys (human login required)'
Same agent quickstart, base URL flowlink.ink
MCP manifest: transport=sse url=https://flowlink.ink/mcp, auth=bearer via SIWE verify
Plain text agent index, base URL flowlink.ink
Confirms pay flow is POST /v1/pay with JWT bearer; settlement streams via SSE on /v1/transactions/{id}/events
404 — OpenAPI only exists on flowlink.ink per llms.txt
